title: Skilled Worker Visa and Work Permit - Germany
summary: Non-EU nationals with a recognized qualification and a job offer in Germany can apply for a skilled worker visa and then convert it into a residence permit for employment.
steps: Obtain a formal job offer from a German employer (Arbeitsvertrag or binding offer letter).
Have your foreign qualification recognized by the relevant German authority (check Anerkennung in Deutschland portal).
Gather all required documents (see list below).
Book an appointment at the German embassy or consulate in your home country (Deutsche Botschaft/Konsulat).
Attend the visa appointment and submit your D-visa application for employment (national visa).
Wait for visa approval (typically 4-12 weeks; embassy will notify you).
Travel to Germany and register your address at the local Einwohnermeldeamt with your landlord confirmation.
Apply for your Residence Permit (Aufenthaltserlaubnis) at the local Foreigners' Office (Auslaenderbehoerde).
Begin work; your employer should register you with the social security system.
documents: Valid passport (Reisepass) - must be valid for the duration of stay
Completed visa application form (Antragsformular fuer ein nationales Visum)
Job offer or employment contract (Arbeitsvertrag oder verbindliches Stellenangebot)
Proof of recognized qualification (Anerkennungsbescheid) or equivalency
University degree and transcripts (Hochschulzeugnis und Transkripte)
Proof of German language skills if required by profession (Deutschkenntnisse)
Blocked account (Sperrkonto) or proof of sufficient funds if no immediate income
office: German Embassy or Consulate in home country (Deutsche Botschaft/Konsulat), then Auslaenderbehoerde in Germany.
fee: EUR75 for national visa; EUR100 for residence permit at Auslaenderbehoerde
notes: The EU Blue Card (Blaue Karte EU) is an alternative for high earners. Salary thresholds and requirements change yearly.
